help.type: The type of simulation batch to run. Currently only 'sensitivity_analysis' is supported, more types may be added later.
help.runs: The number of times that all simulation jobs with the given (range of) argument values should be executed. Set this to 1 if all jobs should run only once (no repetitions).

global explanation: Use the arguments dictionary to specify which arguments should be modified with respect to the defaults defined in config.py, and how. NOTE: If not specified here,
the global random seed will vary between simulations (by default, each sim is given a randomly generated seed).
NOTE: If you want to use a fixed value for the given argument across all simulations, you can simply specify the value (without using a dictionary).

interpolation.global explanation: Use this field to define the kind of interpolation I should use to compute a range of argument values. Currently supported options are listed below.
categorical: Specify 'categorical' if the given value range is already complete (no interpolation needed) and/or if it cannot be interpolated (e.g., a list of strings, such
linear: Use this interpolation type if I should linearly interpolate between the extremes defined in 'range': [<minimum>, <maximum>] with the step size defined in the field 'stepsize'.
The interpolated range will include the given minimum, and if <maximum>-<minimum> is dividable by <stepsize>, then it will also include the maximum.
E.g., range [0.1, 0.4] with stepsize 0.1 will give [0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4], whereas the same range with stepsize 0.12 will give [0.1, 0.22, 0.34]
powerten: Use this interpolation type if I should interpolate between the extremes defined in 'range': [<minimum>, <maximum>] using a power of ten scale with the step size defined in
the field 'stepsize'. The interpolated range will include the given minimum, and if <maximum>-<minimum> is dividable by <stepsize>, then it will also include the maximum.
E.g., range [0, 2] with stepsize 1 will give [10^0, 10^1, 10^2], whereas the same range with stepsize 0.5 will give [10^0, 10^0.5, 10^1, 10^1.5, 10^2]

options per argument.range: Use this field to define the minimum and maximum values for the given argument. The interpretation of these values depends on the interpolation type defined in the field 'interpolation'.
stepsize: Use this field to define the step size for interpolation. The interpretation of this value depends on the interpolation type defined in the
field 'interpolation'. For categorical variables, step size is ignored.
base: In case of dictionary arguments, use this field to define the default value for the given argument. Keys of this dictionary (and any nested dictionaries) will be replaced by 'sub-argument' and
the values associated with it. (with interpolation if specified) to create a range of argument values.
sub_arguments: Use this field if you have a dictionary argument whose nested values you want to vary. Deeply nested keys can be specified using the format 'key1:key2:key3:etc.'
